volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Braggart Soldier (Section 4.7.1310b - Section 4.8.1358)

translation.label: Scaife English translation
translation.citation: Miles Gloriosus, or The Braggart Captain

sections.label: Section 4.7.1310b
sections.translation: See , here they are coming out .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1311
sections.original: Quid modi flendó quaeso hodie fácies ?
sections.translation: Prithee , when will you this day make an end of your weeping ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.1311b
sections.translation: What can I do but weep ? I am going away hence where I have spent my days most happily .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1312b
sections.translation: See , there ’ s the man that has come from your mother and sister .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1313b
sections.translation: I see him .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1313c
sections.translation: Palaestrio , do you hear ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.1314
sections.translation: What is your pleasure ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.1314b
sections.original: Quin tu iubes ecferri ómnia quae isti dedi ?
sections.translation: Aren ’ t you ordering those presents to be brought out which I gave her ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.1315
sections.translation: Health to you , Philocomasium .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1315b
sections.translation: And health to you .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1315c
sections.translation: Your mother and sister bade me give their love to you .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1316b
sections.translation: Heaven prosper them .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1317
sections.original: Orant te , ut eas , ventus operam dum dat , ut velum explicent ;
sections.translation: They beg you to set out , so that , while the wind is fair , they may set sail . But if your mother ’ s eyes had been well , she would have come [*] together with me .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1319
sections.original: Ibo ; quamquam invita facio , impietas sit nisi eam .
sections.translation: I ’ ll go ; although I do it with regret — duty compels me .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1319b
sections.translation: You act wisely .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1320
sections.original: Si non mecum aetatem egisset , hodie stulta viveret .
sections.translation: If she had not been passing her life with myself , this day she would have been a blockhead .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1321
sections.original: Istuc crucior , a viro me tali abalienarier ,
sections.translation: I am distracted at this , that I am estranged from such a man . For you are able to make any woman what-ever abound in wit ; and because I was living with you , for that reason I was of a very lofty spirit . I see that I must lose that loftiness of mind .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1324b
sections.translation: Don ’ t weep .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1324c
sections.translation: I can ’ t help it when I look upon you .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1325b
sections.translation: Be of good courage .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1325c
sections.translation: I know what pain it is to me .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1326
sections.original: Nam nil miror , si libenter , Philocomasium , híc eras ,
sections.translation: I really don ’ t wonder now , Philocomasium , if you were here with happiness to yourself , when I , a servant — as I look at him , weep because we are parting ( pretends to cry ) , so much have his beauty , his manners , his valour , captivated your feelings .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1329
sections.original: Obsecro licet complecti prius quam proficisco ?
sections.translation: Prithee , do let me embrace you before I depart ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.1329b
sections.translation: By all means

sections.label: Section 4.8.1330
sections.original: O mi ócule , o mi ánime .
sections.translation: O my eyes ! O my life !

sections.label: Section 4.8.1330b
sections.translation: Do hold up the woman , I entreat you , lest she should fall .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1331b
sections.translation: What means this ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.1331c
sections.original: Quia abs te abit , animo male
sections.translation: Because , after she had quitted you , she suddenly became faint , poor thing .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1332b
sections.translation: Run in and fetch some water .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1333
sections.original: Nihil aquam moror , quiescat malo . ne interveneris ,
sections.translation: I want no water ; but I had rather you would keep at a distance . Prithee , don ’ t you interfere till she comes to .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1334b
sections.original: Capita inter se nimis nexa hisce habent .
sections.translation: They have their heads too closely in contact between them ;

sections.label: Section 4.8.1335
sections.original: non placet . labra ab labellis aufer , nauta , cave malo .
sections.translation: I don ’ t like it ; he is soldering his lips [*] to hers . What the plague are you about ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.1336
sections.translation: I was trying whether she was breathing or not .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1336b
sections.translation: You ought to have applied your ear then .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1337
sections.translation: If you had rather , I ’ ll let her go .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1337b
sections.translation: No , I don ’ t care ; do you support her .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1337c
sections.translation: To my misery , I ’ m quite distracted .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1338
sections.original: Exite atque ecferte huc intus omnia quae isti dedi .
sections.translation: Go and bring here from in-doors all the things that I have given her .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1339
sections.original: Etiam nunc saluto te , Lar familiaris , prius quam eo .
sections.translation: And even now , household God , do I salute thee before I depart ;

sections.label: Section 4.8.1340
sections.original: conservi conservaeque omnis , bene valete et vivite ,
sections.translation: my fellow-servants , both male and female , all farewell , and happy may you live ; prithee , though absent , among yourselves bestow your blessings upon me as well .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1342
sections.translation: Come , Palaestrio , be of good courage .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1342b
sections.translation: Alas ! alas ! I cannot but weep since from you I must depart .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1343b
sections.translation: Bear it with patience .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1344
sections.original: Sed quid hoc ? quae res ? quid video ? lux , salve ⋯
sections.translation: Ha ! how ’ s this ? What means it ? Hail , O light !

sections.label: Section 4.8.1345
sections.translation: Are you recovered now ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.1345b
sections.translation: Prithee , what person am I embracing ? I ’ m undone . Am I myself ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.1345ab
sections.translation: Fear not , my delight .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1347
sections.translation: What means all this ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.1347b
sections.translation: Just now she swooned away here . ⋯ I fear and dread that this at last may take place [*] too openly .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1349
sections.translation: What is that you say ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.1349b
sections.original: Nos secundum ferri nunc per urbem haec omnia ,
sections.translation: I fear that some one may turn it to your discredit , while all these things are being carried after us through the city .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1350b
sections.translation: I have given away my own property , and not theirs . I care but little for other people . Be off then , go with the blessing of the Gods .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1352
sections.translation: ’ Tis for your sake I say it .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1352b
sections.translation: I believe you .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1352c
sections.translation: And now farewell !

sections.label: Section 4.8.1352d
sections.translation: And heartily farewell to you !

sections.label: Section 4.8.1353
sections.original: Ite cito , iam ego adsequar vos : cúm ero pauca volo loqui . —
sections.translation: Go you quickly on ; I ’ ll overtake you directly ; I wish to speak a few words with my master . ( To PYRGOPOLINICES .) Although you have ever deemed others more faithful to yourself than me ,

sections.label: Section 4.8.1355
sections.original: quam me , tamen tibi habeo magnam gratiam rerum omnium ;
sections.translation: still do I owe you many thanks for all things ; and if such were your feelings , I would rather be a slave to you by far than be the freedman of another .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1357b
sections.translation: Be of good courage .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1358
sections.original: Eheu , quom venit mi in mentem , ut mores mutandi sient ,
sections.translation: Ah me ! When it comes in my mind , how my manners must be changed , how womanish manners must be learnt , and the military ones forgotten !
